name: Bhindi Masala
tagline: Pan-roasted okra with onion-tomato — fiber, slow carbs, no slime
highlight: Okra's soluble fiber slows post-meal glucose; pan-dry first to kill the slime

hero.alt: Sautéed sliced okra coated in onion-tomato masala in a black skillet
hero.caption: bhindi masala — okra crisp, masala fragrant

healthFacts: Okra mucilage is soluble fiber — slows glucose spikes and binds dietary cholesterol.
Low-purine vegetable safe for high uric-acid diets.
Dry-roasting before adding masala collapses the mucilage so it acts inside the gut, not on the plate.

steps.title: Wash, dry, and slice
steps.detail: Wash okra and pat completely dry with a kitchen towel. Trim the heads and slice into 1cm rounds. The drier they are, the less slime later.
steps.tip: If the okra is even slightly damp, the slime will come back — don't rush this step.

steps.title: Pan-roast the okra
steps.detail: Heat 1 tbsp mustard oil in a wide pan. Add okra in a single layer and roast on medium-high, stirring every 2 minutes, for 8–10 minutes until edges crisp and slime disappears.
steps.tip: Don't crowd the pan and don't cover — steam reactivates the slime.

steps.title: Build the masala and combine
steps.detail: Push okra to the side. Add remaining oil, sauté onion until golden, add ginger-garlic and tomato, cook till mushy. Sprinkle spices, salt, and toss with the okra for 3 more minutes.
steps.tip: Finish with a pinch of amchur (dry mango) for a bright tang.
